behavior_summary: U8P_LEGS::look is a simple U8P_LEGS::look/examine handler: when the player inspects this NPC/object it emits a single bark containing the text "legs " and then issues a suspend.
There are no menu interactions, conditionals, flag reads/writes, or external API calls in this snippet; the behavior is a one-shot response to the U8P_LEGS::look event.
notable_control_flow: Entry via the engine's 'U8P_LEGS::look' process (process type 0x0208) triggers the function.
Function executes a single player-facing bark and then immediately suspends the process — no branching or follow-up logic.
The suspend appears to be an engine-level handoff/end of processing rather than custom looping or state machine logic.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "legs " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Is this script attached to an NPC, an object, or an item instance in the game world (the snippet itself does not disambiguate)?

main_function.name: U8P_LEGS::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player's 'U8P_LEGS::look' (examine) action by displaying a short descriptive bark ('legs ') and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or inspects the associated NPC/object (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGS::look
function_map.role: U8P_LEGS::look/examine handler that emits a short bark and suspends
